name: H.P. Lovecraft

acts.description: Wrote explicitly racist poetry and prose; held extreme white supremacist views expressed throughout his work

behavior_summary: Foundational horror writer whose racism was not incidental but central to his creative vision. The monsters in his stories are frequently metaphors for racial anxiety.

art.summary: Invented cosmic horror. His concepts of unknowable cosmic evil have influenced virtually every horror writer since. Cthulhu has become a cultural shorthand.

accountability_events: 2015 World Fantasy Award removed his likeness from the trophy

verdict.blurb: He invented an entire mode of horror built on the terror of encountering the Other — and the Other he was most afraid of was Black people, Jewish people, and anyone not Anglo-Saxon. Cthulhu is basically a metaphor. The award committee eventually noticed.
